response.justification: This candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and teaching ability, but lacks the deep C++ systems programming expertise required. Substantive C++ experience is limited to 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus scattered work, while the role requires 8–12 years of focused development. The past 5+ years have emphasized web technologies (Node.js, TypeScript, React) and AI infrastructure, moving away from systems programming. No demonstrated experience with industrial automation, security standards (SDL/IEC 62443), or industrial protocols required for the position.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals demonstrated through TDD, clean architecture, and mentoring (workshops, Code Katas); proven ability to establish coding standards across teams
Extensive full-stack and DevOps expertise with proficiency in distributed systems, CI/CD, Docker, and infrastructure; shows architectural thinking across multiple domains
Proven ability to rapidly acquire domain expertise and contribute across embedded, web, and distributed systems; open-source leadership and ability to communicate complex concepts
concerns: C++ expertise is severely limited and outdated: only 6 months formal experience at National Instruments (2014–2015), with additional scattered work on robotic arms (2016–2018); no substantive C++ work in past 8+ years

Missing critical domain knowledge: no demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, OPC UA), multi-threaded systems design, or security standards (SDL/IEC 62443)
Career trajectory moves away from required skills: recent 5+ years focused on web technologies and AI infrastructure; no systems programming, embedded development, or secure coding background evident

key_factors.factor: Industrial domain and security standards expertise
Software engineering fundamentals and mentoring capability
